hardcoded-value.description: A computed or configurable value replaced with a hardcoded literal, hiding real behavior behind a constant.

wrong-dispatch.description: A dispatch table, registry, or routing maps an action to the wrong handler or function.

null-safety.description: Code allows None/null values to flow into paths that assume non-null, causing AttributeError or TypeError.

stub-implementation.description: A function body replaced with raise NotImplementedError or pass instead of real logic.

orphan-reference.description: A symbol (class, enum, config key) is deleted but references to it remain, causing ImportError or KeyError at runtime.

incomplete-fix.description: A bug is fixed in one code path but an identical bug remains in a parallel or sibling path.

off-by-one.description: Loop bound, array index, or range off by one, silently skipping the first or last element.

wrong-default.description: A config or parameter default changed to a value that breaks the intended behavior of the system.

removed-security.description: A security check, validation, or enforcement function silently removed during refactoring.

test-skip-abuse.description: Excessive use of pytest.mark.skip or skipif to hide failing tests instead of fixing them.

class-unused.description: A new class or module is added but never instantiated, imported, or referenced anywhere.

infra-no-integration.description: Infrastructure code (config loader, middleware, hook) is created but not wired into the runtime.

config-unused.description: A configuration key is added but no code reads or acts on it.

feature-flag-unchecked.description: A feature flag is defined but the conditional check around the new code path is missing or always true.

api-endpoint-501.description: An API endpoint is registered but returns 501 Not Implemented or raises NotImplementedError.

test-zero-assertions.description: A test function runs code but contains no assert statements, giving false confidence.

test-happy-path-only.description: Tests only cover the success path; no tests for error conditions, edge cases, or invalid input.

test-mocks-subject.description: A test mocks the function under test itself, so the test always passes regardless of real behavior.

test-passes-without-fix.description: A regression test that passes even without the corresponding bug fix applied.

coverage-gamed.description: Code structured to inflate coverage (e.g., executing lines without meaningful assertions).

local-cloud-parity.description: Local development path diverges from cloud/production path in a way that masks bugs.

dev-prod-config.description: Development config differs from production config in ways that cause prod-only failures.

backend-parity.description: Two backend implementations (e.g., MLX vs PyTorch) diverge in behavior or output shape.

rollback-broken.description: A migration or state change has no rollback path, or the rollback path is broken.

error-swallowed.description: An exception is caught with a bare except or except Exception and silently discarded.

filter-over-rejection.description: Invalid data is silently filtered out instead of raising an error, hiding data quality issues.

silent-import-fail.description: An import wrapped in try/except that silently degrades, causing confusing errors later.

metric-never-incremented.description: A metric, counter, or gauge is defined but never incremented or emitted.

log-no-alert.description: An error condition is logged but no alert, metric, or escalation is triggered.

docs-feature-missing.description: Documentation describes a feature that does not exist in the codebase, or vice versa.

config-schema-drift.description: Config file schema changed but documentation or validation schema not updated.

enum-switch-incomplete.description: A new enum variant added but switch/match statements do not handle it, falling through to default.

api-shape-changed.description: API response shape changed but callers not updated, causing KeyError or missing data.

hook-deleted-refactor.description: A hook or callback deleted during refactoring but its registration or caller remains.

identity-spoofing.description: Authentication or authorization check can be bypassed by spoofing identity fields.

bypass-alternative-path.description: A security check exists on the main path but an alternative code path skips it entirely.

secrets-committed.description: API keys, tokens, or passwords committed to source control in plaintext.

race-condition.description: Shared mutable state accessed without synchronization, causing intermittent incorrect behavior.

non-atomic-update.description: A multi-step update that can leave state inconsistent if interrupted between steps.

stale-cache.description: Cache not invalidated after underlying data changes, serving outdated results.

api.description: General API-related code pattern (endpoints, routes, handlers).

endpoint.description: HTTP endpoint addition, modification, or removal.

training-method.description: ML training method configuration or implementation.

tests-updated.description: Tests updated to match code changes.

fallthrough.description: Fallthrough logic in conditionals or switch statements.

scorer.description: Scoring function or metric computation code.

loss-function.description: Loss function implementation for ML training.

dispatch-table.description: Dispatch table or registry mapping actions to handlers.

none-handling.description: Handling of None/null values in data pipelines.

not-implemented.description: NotImplementedError raised in production code path.

deleted-class.description: Class deleted but references remain in codebase.

import-error.description: Import statement that will fail at runtime.

partial-fix.description: Bug fixed in some paths but not all.

concurrency.description: Thread safety, atomicity, and concurrent access patterns.

data-handling.description: Data processing, validation, and transformation patterns.

wiring.description: Component integration, registration, and dependency wiring.

silent-failure.description: Errors, exceptions, or failures that are silently suppressed.

cross-path-parity.description: Divergence between parallel code paths (dev/prod, REST/WS, backends).

documentation-drift.description: Documentation out of sync with actual code behavior.

session-log.description: Sample derived from session log mining.
